Outcome: Feature fully integrated: voice-activated, type-safe, build-passing

user_input: JARVIS keeps saying screen analysis is unavailable even though I have a Gemini key configured
response: Two likely causes: (1) memory poisoning — old failure messages from conversation history get injected into new sessions. Check /api/memory/context for 'unavailable' phrases. (2) wrong key path — IPC handlers reading config.geminiApiKey instead of config.gemini.api_key. Fix: filter poison phrases in get_conversations_context() and use fallback chain config.gemini?.api_key || config.api_key.

user_input: Why does voice work but vision tools say no API key
response: Voice and vision read the API key from different paths. Voice uses config.gemini?.api_key (correct nested path) but vision IPC handlers historically used config.geminiApiKey (camelCase, doesn't exist in standard config). Fix all 4 IPC handlers in desktop/src/main/index.ts to use the fallback chain.

user_input: How do I know if my Gemini quota is exhausted
response: Smoke-test with curl/python POST to https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=YOUR_KEY. HTTP 429 with 'limit: 0' means free tier exhausted. Or check https://ai.google.dev/rate-limit dashboard.

user_input: How can I make screen analysis work without Gemini API
response: Use Ollama with a vision-capable model. gemma3:4b is multimodal and runs in ~3GB VRAM. Endpoint: GET /api/screen/analyze on Python backend uses pyautogui for capture and Ollama /api/generate for analysis. Zero internet, zero quota.

Check via POST /api/show with the model name and look for 'vision' in the response.capabilities array. Currently vision-capable: gemma3:4b (multimodal native), llava:7b, llava:13b, llama3.2-vision:11b, moondream, bakllava. Text-only: llama3.2, gemma2, jarvis-brain.

user_input: JARVIS keeps reconnecting after a research call
response: The research IPC call hangs longer than Gemini Live's server-side keepalive timeout. Wrap api.jarvisResearch() in a 20s Promise.race timeout in JarvisGeminiLive.ts so failed research returns gracefully instead of dropping the WebSocket.

user_input: I see 'null' as a JARVIS response in the transcript
response: Backend /api/chat returned reply: null and the renderer rendered it literally. Two-part fix: (1) server.py — coerce null reply to a placeholder before responding. (2) DashboardView.tsx — filter messages where text is null/empty before rendering.

user_input: Paste isn't working in the JARVIS input box
response: Electron's clipboard security context can block native Ctrl+V in some configurations. Add an explicit onPaste handler to the textarea that calls e.clipboardData.getData('text') and inserts at the cursor position.

user_input: What's the fastest way to read text on my screen
response: Tesseract OCR is ~50ms vs ~2s for a vision LLM, and more accurate for pure text. Use it as Tier 0 above the vision LLM. For 'describe what's on screen' queries that need reasoning, pair OCR text + image to gemma3:4b.

user_input: How do I check what Gemini Live tools JARVIS knows about
response: Read desktop/src/renderer/src/services/generatedToolDeclarations.json — that's what's pushed to the Gemini Live setup turn. If a tool isn't there, the model literally cannot call it. Regenerate from canonical TOOL_SCHEMAS via python core/tool_export.py.

user_input: How can I make JARVIS work fully offline
response: Replace each cloud dependency with a local equivalent: Vision -> Ollama gemma3:4b. STT -> faster-whisper. TTS -> Piper. LLM brain -> already Ollama. Wake word -> Picovoice Porcupine. Web search -> DuckDuckGo HTML scrape. Keep cloud as fallback, not default.

user_input: JARVIS forgets I said something earlier in the conversation
response: Check core/database.py get_conversations_context() — it limits to max_exchanges=15. With aggressive poison filtering, the actual injected count can be lower. Increase the limit or add explicit memory entries via /api/memory/save.

user_input: Why does Gemini Live still work when REST API quota is dead
response: Gemini Live runs on the BidiGenerateContent WebSocket API which has a separate quota allocation from the REST generateContent endpoint. Different daily limit, different RPM. Voice keeps working even when REST is exhausted.

user_input: How do I detect that local Ollama vision is available
response: GET http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags returns installed models. Then POST /api/show with each model name and check response.capabilities for 'vision'. The Python helper _pick_local_vision_model() in web/server.py does this with a preference order.

user_input: Tools work when typed but voice JARVIS won't call them
response: The Gemini Live model only sees tools in generatedToolDeclarations.json. Even if the switch handler in JarvisGeminiLive.ts has a case for it, the model never tries it because the tool declaration is missing. Run python core/tool_export.py to regenerate.
